definition: A playing card worth 1 or 11 points in blackjack, 1 in baccarat, and the highest card in poker
details: In BJ it can be counted as 1 or 11 without busting. In baccarat and poker it's worth 1 point and the highest rank respectively.

definition: (1) The total amount wagered over a period of time (2) A player's turn to bet
details: Casinos track 'action' for comp calculations. A player with $10,000 of action across a weekend might receive complimentary rooms and meals.

definition: To bet all of one's remaining chips in a single wager
details: When a player goes all-in, a side pot is created for remaining players who have chips. The all-in player can only win the main pot.

definition: A forced bet posted by all players before the start of a hand
details: Antes are more common in tournament poker and stud games, creating action by increasing the pot size before cards are even dealt.

definition: One of the two hands in baccarat (Banco); also the player who banks the game
details: In Punto Banco, the Banker hand is simply a designation. The house charges a 5% commission on winning Banker bets, giving it a 1.06% edge.

definition: The total amount of money a player has set aside for gambling
details: Proper bankroll management is essential. Most experts recommend having at least 50-100x the minimum bet for table games.

definition: The mathematically optimal way to play every possible blackjack hand combination
details: Basic strategy was first published in 1956 by Baldwin, Cantey, Maisel, and McDermott. For a standard 6-deck H17 game, the house edge is approximately 0.66%.

definition: The larger of two forced bets in Hold'em, typically equal to the minimum bet
details: Posted by the player to the left of the small blind. In tournament play, blinds increase at regular intervals to force action.

definition: A starting hand of an Ace plus a 10-value card (10/J/Q/K), paying 3:2 traditionally
details: Blackjack occurs about 4.8% of the time. A 6:5 blackjack payout increases the house edge by approximately 1.4%.

definition: Forced bets posted before cards are dealt, creating initial action in the pot
details: The two blinds (small and big) ensure there is always money to play for. Their positions rotate clockwise each hand via the dealer button.

definition: Betting or raising with a weak hand to induce opponents to fold superior hands
details: Successful bluffing requires understanding opponent tendencies, table image, and board texture. Bluff frequency should be balanced with value bets.

definition: The seated casino employee managing chips and supervising dealers at the craps table
details: The boxman sits behind the chip rack, verifies payouts, handles coloring up players, and watches for dealer or player errors.

definition: The point in a tournament where the next eliminated player finishes out of the money
details: Players on the bubble often play extremely tight, as one more elimination guarantees at least a minimum cash. This creates opportunities for aggressive players.

definition: The top card of the deck discarded before dealing community cards to prevent marking
details: One card is burned before the flop, turn, and river. This prevents any player from seeing the back of the next card to be dealt.

definition: When a hand exceeds 21 points, resulting in an automatic loss
details: The dealer busts approximately 28% of the time depending on rules. Players who understand basic strategy minimize their own bust rate.

definition: A wager on a point number paying true odds less a 5% commission
details: Buy bets are superior to Place bets on the 4 and 10 (1.19% vs 1.67% house edge). The commission is often charged only on winning bets.

definition: The amount of money a player pays to enter a tournament or sit at a cash game table
details: Tournament buy-ins go toward prize pools (minus the house rake). Cash game buy-ins are converted to chips of equal value.

definition: To match the current bet amount, staying in the hand
details: Calling is generally weaker than raising in most situations. Frequent calling without raising is known as being a 'calling station.'

definition: Tracking dealt cards to identify situations where the remaining deck favors the player
details: The Hi-Lo system assigns +1 to 2-6, 0 to 7-9, and -1 to 10-A. A true count of +1 gives the player approximately a 0.5% advantage. Card counting is legal but casinos reserve the right to refuse service.

definition: To pass the action without betting, available only when no prior bet has been made
details: Checking preserves the option to raise later. The 'check-raise' is a powerful move where a player checks initially then raises after an opponent bets.

definition: A round token used as currency in casinos, technically a 'check' when bearing a denomination
details: Roulette chips have no printed value — each player receives a unique color. Standard American chip values: White=$1, Red=$5, Green=$25, Black=$100.

definition: A deck that seems to produce consistently poor hands for a player
details: 'Cold deck' is a superstitious term. In reality, card distribution is random and 'cold' streaks are expected in any game of chance.

definition: A bet made after the point is established, functioning like a Pass bet starting from the next roll
details: Come bets are placed in the 'Come' area. The next roll serves as a mini-come-out for that bet. Odds can be taken behind Come bets.

definition: The first roll of a craps round, or any roll before a point is established
details: The puck shows 'OFF' during come-out. Pass bets win on 7/11 and lose on 2/3/12. The come-out roll gives Pass bets much of their value.

definition: A fee charged by the casino on certain bets, typically 5%, also known as the vigorish or vig
details: Commission is standard on Banker bets in baccarat (5%) and Buy/Lay bets in craps (5%). Some casinos collect commission only on winning bets.

definition: Face-up cards shared by all players to build their best five-card hand
details: In Texas Hold'em, 5 community cards are dealt: 3 on the flop, 1 on the turn, and 1 on the river. Players use any combination of their 2 hole cards and 5 community cards.

definition: Complimentary items or services given to players based on their action or theoretical loss
details: Comps include free rooms, meals, show tickets, and cashback. Casinos typically comp about 30-40% of a player's expected loss.

definition: The French term for a casino dealer, responsible for conducting table games
details: Originally meaning 'one who rode behind on horseback,' referring to someone who stood behind a gambler with extra cash. In Europe, croupiers typically learn roulette first; in North America, blackjack.

definition: A colored plastic card placed in the shoe to indicate the last round before reshuffling
details: Typically placed about 1-1.5 decks from the bottom. When reached, the current hand is the last round before the shuffle. This protects against card counting.

definition: A marker showing the dealer position, rotating clockwise each hand in poker
details: The button determines the order of action. In Hold'em, the player on the button acts last post-flop, a positional advantage.

definition: The opposite of a Come bet — winning when 7 appears before the specified number
details: Like Come but betting against the shooter. Bar-12 or bar-2 (varies by casino) results in a push on the first roll for Don't Come bets.

definition: A bet that the shooter will lose, winning on 2/3 on come-out, pushing on 12
details: Don't Pass has a slightly lower house edge (1.36%) than Pass (1.41%). Players betting Don't are called 'wrong bettors.'

definition: In stud games, the first face-up card dealt to each player
details: In 7-card stud, each player receives 2 down cards and 1 up card as their door card, which is the basis for early betting decisions.

definition: A player option to double the original wager in exchange for exactly one additional card
details: Double down is most advantageous when the player has a hard 10 or 11, as drawing a 10-value card gives a very strong 20 or 21.

definition: The second green pocket on American roulette wheels, giving the house a 5.26% edge
details: European roulette has only one zero (2.70% edge). The double zero was introduced in the United States and remains unique to American wheels.

definition: A card dealt face-down, visible only to the player or dealer who receives it
details: In blackjack, the dealer's down card is called the 'hole card.' In poker, all initial cards are dealt face-down as 'hole cards.'

definition: A hand that requires additional cards to become a strong made hand
details: Common draws: flush draw (9 outs), open-ended straight draw (8 outs), gutshot (4 outs). Players calculate 'pot odds' to determine if chasing a draw is profitable.

definition: The casino's mathematical advantage as a percentage of each wager
details: House edge varies greatly: BJ basic strategy (0.5-1%), European roulette (2.70%), American roulette (5.26%), slots (2-15%), keno (up to 35%).

definition: A 1:1 payout — the player receives an amount equal to their original wager
details: Also called 'blackjack even money' when a player with blackjack takes insurance against a dealer ace, guaranteeing a 1:1 payout instead of risking a push.

definition: The ceiling-mounted surveillance camera system used to monitor casino activity
details: Recordings are used to resolve disputes, identify cheating, and protect both players and the casino. All table actions, especially hand signals, must be clearly visible to cameras.

definition: A one-roll bet on 2, 3, 4, 9, 10, 11, or 12, usually with bonus payouts on 2 and 12
details: Best odds when 2 pays 3:1 and 12 pays 3:1 (2.78% house edge). Worst when only 2:1 on both (5.56%). Some casinos offer 3:1 on both for 0% edge.

definition: The first three community cards dealt face-up in Texas Hold'em
details: After the flop, each player has seen approximately 71% of their final hand (2 hole cards + 3 community cards out of 7 total).

definition: To abandon one's hand and forfeit any chips already wagered
details: Folding is a critical skill — knowing when to minimize losses is as important as knowing when to bet. Professional players fold the majority of starting hands.

definition: To play long sessions of poker or table games, building bankroll slowly through consistent small wins
details: 'Grinders' are players who make a steady income through volume and discipline rather than seeking big scores. Common among professional poker players.

definition: A hand without an Ace, or with an Ace forced to count as 1 to prevent busting
details: Hard 12-16 are the most difficult decisions in basic strategy. The dealer upcard significantly influences the correct play.

definition: A bet that an even number (4/6/8/10) will be rolled with both dice showing identical values
details: Hard 6/8 pays 9:1 (US), hard 4/10 pays 7:1. A 7 or an 'easy' way (different dice values) causes the bet to lose. House edge is 2.78%.

definition: A player who wagers large amounts of money, often receiving VIP treatment
details: High rollers receive luxury comps: private jets, penthouse suites, personal hosts, and negotiated rebates on losses. Casinos compete aggressively for these players.

definition: To request an additional card from the dealer
details: The hand signal for hit is tapping the table with a finger (face-up games) or scraping cards against the table (handheld games).

definition: The dealer's face-down card, checked when the upcard is a 10-value card or Ace
details: In US casinos, the dealer peeks at the hole card when showing an Ace or 10. If they have blackjack, the hand ends immediately.

definition: A one-roll bet divided equally among 2, 3, 11, and 12
details: Common in craps, the horn bet is placed in the center of the table. 'Horn high' doubles the bet on one specific number (e.g., horn high 12).

definition: The amount a player expects to win on future betting rounds if they make their draw
details: Implied odds justify chasing draws when the pot isn't offering sufficient direct pot odds but future bets will compensate if the draw hits.

definition: A side bet offered when the dealer shows an Ace, paying 2:1 if the dealer has blackjack
details: Insurance is mathematically a losing proposition in the long run. Basic strategy dictates never taking insurance, regardless of the player's hand.

definition: A side card not part of the primary hand ranking, used to break ties between equal hands
details: If two players both have a pair of Aces, the player with the higher kicker (side card) wins. Example: A-A-K beats A-A-Q.

definition: A wager that a 7 will be rolled before a specific number, paying true odds minus commission
details: Lay bets are the opposite of Buy bets. Laying the 4 pays 1:2 (bet $20 to win $10). Commission is based on the win amount, typically 5%.

definition: A betting structure where raises are fixed at predetermined amounts
details: In $2/$4 limit Hold'em, bets and raises are $2 pre-flop and on the flop, then $4 on the turn and river. This limits both risk and reward.

definition: (1) A formal casino credit instrument (2) A token marking a winning number on roulette
details: Casino markers are essentially short-term loans that must be repaid within 30 days or risk prosecution. The roulette marker is called a 'dolly.'

definition: A betting system where the wager is doubled after each loss, theoretically recovering losses with a single win
details: The Martingale system fails due to table limits and finite bankrolls. A losing streak of 7-8 consecutive bets quickly reaches unaffordable levels.

definition: An instant-winning hand or roll, such as blackjack (21) in blackjack or 7/11 in craps
details: In baccarat, a natural is 8 or 9 on the first two cards. In blackjack, only an Ace with a 10-value card on the initial deal is a natural blackjack.

definition: A betting structure where a player may bet any amount up to their entire stack at any time
details: No-Limit Texas Hold'em is the most popular poker format, featured in the WSOP Main Event. It allows maximum strategic depth and risk.

definition: A supplementary wager behind the Pass/Don't Pass line that pays true mathematical odds
details: Odds bets have exactly 0% house edge. The standard is 3-4-5X odds, meaning 3X on 4/10, 4X on 5/9, and 5X on 6/8.

definition: A bet on grouped categories (red/black, odd/even, high/low, dozens, columns) with higher win probability
details: Outside bets cover 12-18 numbers, giving significantly better odds of winning but lower payouts (1:1 or 2:1).

definition: The fundamental craps bet — wins on 7/11 come-out, loses on 2/3/12, requires point repetition thereafter
details: The Pass bet has a 1.41% house edge with 3-4-5X odds reducing the combined edge to just 0.37%. Nearly every shooter bets the Pass line.

definition: The percentage of cards dealt from the shoe before reshuffling
details: Deeper penetration favors card counters. A shoe with 75% penetration (1.5 decks cut from 6) is considered good for advantage players.

definition: A casino supervisor who oversees multiple tables and handles disputes or unusual situations
details: The pit boss can authorize credit, approve larger bets, rate players for comps, and resolve rule disputes. They report to the shift manager.

definition: A bet on a specific point number (4/5/6/8/9/10) winning if rolled before a 7
details: Place bets pay less than true odds: 6/8 pay 7:6 (0.46% edge), 5/9 pay 7:5 (1.11%), 4/10 pay 9:5 (1.67%). Best value is on 6 and 8.

definition: One of the two hands (Punto) in baccarat, or a person betting at a casino game
details: In Punto Banco, the Player hand is not the individual gambler — it's simply one of two dealt hands. Players can bet on either or both hands.

definition: The number (4/5/6/8/9/10) established on the come-out roll that determines continued play
details: Once the point is set, the puck is turned to 'ON' and placed on that number. The shooter continues rolling until they hit the point or seven-out.

definition: A family of card games where players wager on the strength of their hand, with elements of skill and psychology
details: There are hundreds of poker variants, with Texas Hold'em being the most popular worldwide. Poker differs from other casino games as players compete against each other, not the house.

definition: The total amount of chips wagered by all players in a single poker hand
details: The pot is awarded to the player with the best hand at showdown, or the last player remaining after all others fold. Side pots are created when a player goes all-in.

definition: The ratio of the current pot size to the cost of a contemplated call
details: Pot odds help players determine whether chasing a draw is mathematically profitable. If pot odds are greater than the odds of making the draw, calling is profitable long-term.

definition: A one-roll wager on specific dice outcomes, typically found in the center of the craps table
details: Prop bets include Any 7 (16.67% edge), Any Craps (11.11%), and specific numbers like 2 or 12. These have the highest house edge in craps.

definition: A tie between bettor and house, resulting in the original wager being returned without profit or loss
details: In blackjack, a push occurs when the player and dealer have equal totals. In craps, Don't Pass bets push on 12. The bet is neither won nor lost.

definition: A low-value or non-threatening community card unlikely to help any player's hand
details: A 'rag flop' might consist of low, offsuit, disconnected cards like 2-7-9 rainbow. Such flops often favor players who raised pre-flop.

definition: The spectators watching a poker game, often used in 'railbird' (a spectator who follows the action)
details: During major tournaments, the rail is packed with fans. 'Railing' a player means watching and supporting them from the sidelines.

definition: To increase the current bet amount, requiring all subsequent players to match the new amount to continue
details: Raising serves multiple purposes: building the pot with a strong hand, isolating weaker players, bluffing, and gaining information about opponents' holdings.

definition: A commission fee collected by the casino from each cash game pot or as a tournament entry fee
details: Typical rake is 5% of the pot up to a maximum (e.g., $3-$5). Rake affects profitability — professional players must overcome both opponents and the rake.

definition: The fifth and final community card in Texas Hold'em, also called fifth street
details: The river is the last chance to improve a hand. 'Rivered' means making a hand on the river, as in 'I rivered a flush.'

definition: The unadjusted tally of card values in Hi-Lo counting (starting from 0 with a fresh shoe)
details: Running Count is converted to True Count by dividing by the estimated number of decks remaining. True Count = Running Count / Remaining Decks.

definition: When the shooter rolls a 7 after establishing a point, ending the round and passing the dice
details: A seven-out is the most dramatic moment in craps. All Pass line bets lose, Don't Pass bets win, and a new shooter receives the dice.

definition: A device holding multiple decks from which the dealer pushes cards out one at a time
details: Standard shoes hold 4, 6, or 8 decks. Some casinos use continuous shuffle machines (CSM) that shuffle cards after every hand, negating card counting.

definition: The player currently throwing the dice in craps
details: The shooter must bet on Pass or Don't Pass. Dice must hit the opposite wall, be thrown with one hand, and both dice must be thrown simultaneously.

definition: The final phase where remaining players reveal their hole cards to determine the winner
details: At showdown, the best five-card hand wins. Players typically show cards in order of the last better or raiser. Mucking (discarding) is an option.

definition: A separate pot created when a player goes all-in, containing bets from remaining active players
details: Multiple side pots can exist in a single hand. Each side pot is awarded separately. An all-in player can only win the main pot, not any side pots.

definition: The smaller forced bet posted by the player immediately clockwise from the dealer
details: Typically half the size of the big blind. In tournaments, the small blind is often rounded up (e.g., 100/200 blinds, small blind pays 100).

definition: A hand containing an Ace that can be counted as 11 without exceeding 21
details: Soft hands are flexible — the Ace can be reduced from 11 to 1 if an additional card would cause a bust. This gives the player more options.

definition: To separate a pair into two independent hands by matching the original bet
details: Aces should always be split (gives two shots at 21). Never split 10s (you already have 20). 8s should be split against any dealer upcard except Ace.

definition: The amount of money a player risks on a single bet or session
details: 'Staking' also refers to an arrangement where a backer funds a player in exchange for a percentage of potential winnings.

definition: To decline additional cards and play the current hand
details: The hand signal is waving a hand horizontally palm-down over the cards (face-up games), or sliding cards under the chips (handheld games).

definition: The casino employee who controls the dice with a curved stick, announces rolls, and manages center bets
details: The stickman uses distinctive verbal patter to announce results, encourages betting, and maintains the pace of the game.

definition: A bet on a single number in roulette, offering the highest payout at 35:1
details: Straight up bets have the lowest probability of winning (2.63% American, 2.70% European) but offer the largest potential return.

definition: One of four categories in a standard deck: spades, hearts, diamonds, or clubs
details: In most casino games, suits are equal in value. Poker uses suits only for flush/straight flush rankings. Bridge uses them for trump determination.

definition: To forfeit half the bet and end the hand immediately on the first two cards
details: Late surrender (offered after dealer checks for blackjack) reduces house edge by 0.08%. Early surrender (before dealer checks) is very rare and more advantageous.

definition: The fixed set of rules determining whether a third card must be dealt in Punto Banco
details: The tableau removes all player decision-making from baccarat — it's a pure game of chance. Players simply bet on Player, Banker, or Tie.

definition: A physical or behavioral clue that reveals the strength of a player's hand
details: Classic tells: shaking hands (nervousness with a bluff), staring at cards (interest in a strong hand), sudden stillness (usually indicates strength).

definition: A state of emotional frustration causing a player to make irrational, often costly decisions
details: Tilt is considered the single biggest bankroll killer in poker. Common triggers: bad beats, losing sessions, exhaustion. Recognizing tilt is critical for serious players.

definition: A player's request for extra time to make a decision; also a form of rake where players pay by the hour
details: In tournament poker, players may request 'time' (usually 30-60 seconds). 'Time rake' or 'seat charge' is common in high-stakes games instead of per-pot rake.

definition: The fourth community card in Texas Hold'em, also known as fourth street
details: The turn is dealt after flop betting concludes. It significantly changes hand values and often alters betting dynamics.

definition: The dealer's face-up card visible to all players, forming the basis of basic strategy decisions
details: The upcard is the most important visible information. The dealer's upcard determines the basic strategy for every player hand.

definition: The statistical measure of short-term fluctuations in results around the expected long-term average
details: High variance games (craps, poker) produce larger swings in bankroll. Low variance games (baccarat banker bet) provide more predictable results.

definition: The commission or fee charged by the casino on certain bets, usually 5%
details: The vigorish ensures the house has a mathematical edge. Common examples: baccarat banker bet (5%), craps buy/lay bets (5%), sports betting (10% juice).

definition: The initial mixing of new cards by spreading them face-down and swirling on the table
details: New decks are 'washed' before the first shuffle to randomize the factory order. After washing, cards are gathered into a deck for cutting and then shuffling.

definition: An extremely high-spending casino customer, capable of wagering millions of dollars per session
details: Whales receive the highest level of VIP treatment: private gaming salons, dedicated hosts, luxury transportation, and substantial loss rebates.

definition: A five-way one-roll bet divided equally among 2, 3, 7, 11, and 12
details: The World bet combines Any Craps (2,3,12) plus the 7 and 11. It covers 7 out of 36 possible dice combinations.

definition: The single green pocket on European roulette wheels, providing the house its 2.70% edge
details: All roulette bets lose if the ball lands in zero, except special rules like La Partage and En Prison which give half back on even-money bets.
